description: Creates an alias for an existing type, making code more readable and easier to maintain. Alkyl supports a modern syntax that makes complex types (like function pointers) clean and readable.
features: Modern and clean function pointer type definitions.
constraints: Use `typedef AliasName as OriginalType;` instead of the legacy C syntax.
Function pointer types can use the clean `return_type(param_types)` syntax instead of `return_type(*)(param_types)`.
